ground.cypher:
MATCH
    p1 = (a:Character {name: 'Hamlet'})-[r1:INTERACTS45]->(b:Character),
    p2 = (a)-[r2:INTERACTS]->(c:Character {name: 'Ophelia'}),
    p3 = (b)-[r3:INTERACTS3]->(d:Character {name: 'Polonius'})
RETURN p1, p2, p3
ground.question: How does Hamlet’s centrality in *Hamlet* compare to his interactions with Ophelia and Polonius in terms of the weighted influence across these three distinct relationships?

cypher: MATCH (h:Character {name: 'Hamlet'})-[:INTERACTS1]->(o:Character {name: 'Ophelia'}),       (h)-[:INTERACTS2]->(p:Character {name: 'Polonius'}),       (h)-[:INTERACTS3]->(q:Character)\nRETURN h.centrality,       SUM((h)-[:INTERACTS1]->(o).weight) AS interacts1,       SUM((h)-[:INTERACTS2]->(p).weight) AS interacts2,       SUM((h)-[:INTERACTS3]->(q).weight) AS interacts3
